title: sorbus ambigua
intro: Sorbus ambigua, also known as the Chinese rowan or Chinese mountain ash, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to China and the Himalayas. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, with its bark and leaves containing flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids that possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The medicinal properties of Sorbus ambigua have been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating pain, and treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. In herbal preparations, Sorbus ambigua is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, and can also be used in topical applications to soothe skin irritations.
